---
title: "Research"
subtitle: "Publications, working papers, and ongoing projects"
toc: true
toc-location: right
---

## Research themes

The lab organises its work around four overlapping themes. Most projects sit at
the intersection of two or more.

- **Macroeconomic policy** — fiscal, monetary, and growth diagnostics.
- **Trade & industrial policy** — CGE modeling (GTAP-RD/GDyn-FS), tariff
  analysis, structural transformation.
- **Applied econometrics** — panel methods, gravity, and causal inference for
  Indonesian micro and macro data.
- **Climate & energy** — distributional effects of carbon pricing and the
  green transition in developing economies.

## Published work

::: {.publication}
[Trade Liberalisation and Household Welfare in Indonesia: Evidence from District-Level Panel Data]{.pub-title}

[Krisna Gupta, Dinda Rachmania, Arya Pratama]{.pub-authors}

[Journal of Development Economics · 2024]{.pub-meta}

[We exploit variation in tariff reductions to identify the causal effect of trade openness on household consumption and poverty rates across 500+ Indonesian districts from 2000–2020.]{.pub-abstract}

[Trade]{.tag} [Indonesia]{.tag} [Causal Inference]{.tag}
:::

::: {.publication}
[Fiscal Decentralisation, Local Governance Quality, and Public Service Delivery]{.pub-title}

[Siti Aminah, Budi Santoso, Krisna Gupta]{.pub-authors}

[World Development · 2024]{.pub-meta}

[Using a regression-discontinuity design around population thresholds that determine local government tiers, we estimate the effect of decentralisation on health and education outcomes.]{.pub-abstract}

[Fiscal Policy]{.tag} [Public Finance]{.tag} [RDD]{.tag}
:::

::: {.publication}
[Carbon Pricing in a Developing Economy: Labour Market and Distributional Effects]{.pub-title}

[Dinda Rachmania, Yudo Prabowo]{.pub-authors}

[Energy Policy · 2023]{.pub-meta}

[A CGE model calibrated to the Indonesian input-output table assesses how alternative carbon-pricing schemes affect wages, employment, and income distribution across sectors.]{.pub-abstract}

[Climate]{.tag} [CGE]{.tag} [Labour Economics]{.tag}
:::

::: {.publication}
[Long-run Effects of the Indonesia–US ART on Bilateral Trade Flows]{.pub-title}

[Krisna Gupta, Arya Pratama]{.pub-authors}

[Asian Economic Journal · 2023]{.pub-meta}

[Gravity estimation with `ppmlhdfe` and three-way fixed effects, using HS-6
product-level trade data over 2000–2022.]{.pub-abstract}

[Trade]{.tag} [Gravity]{.tag} [ASEAN]{.tag}
:::

## Working papers

::: {.publication}
[Heterogeneous Pass-through of Exchange-rate Shocks in Indonesian Manufacturing]{.pub-title}

[DEN Working Paper · 2026]{.pub-meta}

[A heterogeneous-agent New-Keynesian model with imported intermediate inputs,
calibrated to Indonesian firm-level data, to quantify the role of input
exposure in exchange-rate pass-through.]{.pub-abstract}

[Macro]{.tag} [HANK]{.tag} [Pass-through]{.tag}
:::

::: {.publication}
[Climate-Aware GTAP: Extending GDyn-FS for Indonesia's NDC]{.pub-title}

[DEN Working Paper · 2026]{.pub-meta}

[A dynamic CGE extension with sector-specific abatement curves, applied to
Indonesia's Nationally Determined Contribution under different financing
assumptions.]{.pub-abstract}

[Climate]{.tag} [CGE]{.tag} [GTAP]{.tag}
:::

## Active projects

| Project | Lead | Partners | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| Industrial policy & downstream value chains | K. Gupta | Bappenas, ERIA | Ongoing |
| Indonesia–US ART impact assessment | A. Pratama | DEN | Ongoing |
| Carbon pricing & just transition | D. Rachmania | World Bank | Year 1 of 3 |
| Digital financial inclusion in rural Indonesia | S. Aminah | OJK | Field work |

## Open-source tooling

We maintain a small set of open-source tools to keep our analysis reproducible
and consistent across team members:

- [**fig-den**](https://github.com/den-econ/fig-den) — matplotlib styling and
  chart helpers in DEN house style.
- [**ggden**](https://github.com/den-econ/ggden) — the ggplot2 equivalent for R.
- [**wp**](https://github.com/den-econ/wp) — Quarto template for working papers.
- [**deck**](https://github.com/den-econ/deck) — Quarto Beamer template for
  slide decks.

All lab repositories live at [github.com/den-econ](https://github.com/den-econ).
